content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 28, 1991

Frances A. Moore, CMC
City Clerk
City of Alhambra
111 South First Street
Alhambra, CA  91801

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-91-107

Dear Ms. Moore:

     You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on behalf of two primarily formed committees in your
jurisdiction, the Help Our City Committee and the Alhambra
Citizens Against Crime Committee, concerning the personal use
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

     This letter confirms that your letter dated February 15,
1991, accurately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you
on that date.  As we discussed in our telephone conversation, the
Act requires that expenditures of campaign funds by committees,
whether candidate controlled or not, be consistent with the Act's
restrictions on personal use of campaign funds.

     Section 89511 of the Act provides:

          (a)  This article applies to campaign funds
     held by candidates for elective office, elected
     officers, controlled committees, ballot measure
     committees, committees opposed to a candidate or
     measure, political action committees, and any
     committee which qualifies as a committee pursuant
     to subdivision (a) of Section 82013.

_______________________________

1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-91-107
Page 2


(b) (1) For purposes of this chapter,
"campaign funds" includes any contributions, cash,
cash equivalents, and other assets received or
possessed by a committee as defined by subdivision
(a) of Section 82013.

[Emphasis added.]

The general rule of the new personal use law is that any
expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a minimum, reasonably
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.27
(Section 89512.) However, where an expenditure confers a
substantial personal benefit on the candidate or other controlling
person, the expenditure must be directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.3/ (Section 89512.)

Thus, I advised you that provided the expenditure confers no
personal benefit on any person involved in the decisionmaking
process of the committee making the transfer, and the expenditure
is reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose, the expenditure is permissible.4/ (Section 89512.) As I
stated in our telephone conversation, the transfer to another
ballot measure committee in the jurisdiction appears to be
reasonably related to a political purpose and would therefore be
permissible.


___________________________

2/ Moreover, where campaign funds become surplus campaign funds,
the personal use law further limits their expenditure. (Section
89519; Regulation 18587, copy enclosed.) However, Section 89519
expressly applies the definition of "surplus funds" to candidates
and their committees. While other sections of the personal use
law have been interpreted more broadly to apply to committees and
candidates, the surplus funds provision has historically been
interpreted to apply to candidates and their controlled committees
only.

3/ A "substantial personal benefit" is defined as a direct
personal benefit to the candidate of $100 or more. (Section
89511(b)(2); Regulation 18580, copy enclosed.)

4/ For example, if the money transferred to Committee 2 was to be
used to pay a salary of $100 or more to a person who was also a
controlling member of the transferrer, the direct relationship
standard would be appropriate.

Our File No. A-91-107
Page 3


Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa

Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: Whether expenditures of campaign funds by two ballot measure committees in Alhambra for transfers to other committees comply with the Personal Reform Act's restrictions on personal use of campaign funds.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign fund expenditures are permissible if they confer no personal benefit on decision-makers and are reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. The transfer to another ballot measure committee in the jurisdiction appears reasonably related to a political purpose and would therefore be permissible. The surplus funds provision applies only to candidates and their controlled committees, not to other committees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation of prior telephone advice rather than response to written question. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present, but legal analysis clearly provided. Text quality issues in OCR (special characters, formatting problems) but content extractable. File number A-91-107 confirmed in header and footer.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether expenditures of campaign funds by two ballot measure committees in Alhambra for transfers to other committees comply with the Personal Reform Act's restrictions on personal use of campaign funds.

CONCLUSION: Campaign fund expenditures are permissible if they confer no personal benefit on decision-makers and are reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. The transfer to another ballot measure committee in the jurisdiction appears reasonably related to a political purpose and would therefore be permissible. The surplus funds provision applies only to candidates and their controlled committees, not to other committees.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding personal use restrictions on campaign funds held by two ballot measure committees (Help Our City Committee and Alhambra Citizens Against Crime Committee), explaining that expenditures must be reasonably related to political purposes and advising that a proposed transfer to another ballot measure committee would be permissible.
